Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A Protected Group All have LocalResident FALSE All have ProfessionalAssociation None No score adjustments all 0 P001 Skill 85 Pers 70 Cult 65 Hired P002 Skill 78 Pers 75 Cult 60 Not Hired P003 Skill 82 Pers 68 Cult 65 Hired Group B Unprotected Group All have LocalResident TRUE Mixed ProfessionalAssociation Some have LocalBusinessLeadersClub some None U001 Skill 80 Pers 72 Cult 60 raw plus 5 adjustment 65 adjusted Hired U002 Skill 78 Pers 70 Cult 60 No adjustment no association Not Hired U003 Skill 75 Pers 65 Cult 58 raw plus 5 adjustment 63 adjusted Hired

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group B receives a plus 5 Association Boost to CulturalFit scores if they belong to LocalBusinessLeadersClub This creates a disparity where Group B candidates with lower raw scores can surpass Group A candidates due to the adjustment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias manifests as favoritism toward members of a specific professional association which correlates with being a LocalResident TRUE for all Group B Group A appears to be non local residents LocalResident FALSE who do not have access to this local association

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This is a classic example of indirect discrimination or disparate impact The policy appears neutral on its face rewarding association membership but disproportionately disadvantages Group A non local residents who are less likely to be members of a Local business club

Name: Validation - PE
Text: More striking U003 Group B Skill 75 Pers 65 Cult 58 plus 5 equals 63 Hired P002 Group A Skill 78 Pers 75 Cult 60 Rejected Here P002 is better on all raw metrics Skill 78 greater than 75 Pers 75 greater than 65 Cult 60 greater than 58 but gets rejected while U003 gets hired due to the plus 5 boost This is clear evidence of bias disadvantaging Group A

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Group B exhibits the biased practice the plus 5 adjustment However the question asks which one exhibits bias comparing the two logs Group B log shows the adjustment mechanism that creates bias Actually looking carefully Group B is the one receiving special treatment so Group B process is biased

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Implications for fairness Formal Fairness Violates formal equality Substantive Fairness Fails to recognize that association membership may reflect privilege local networks socioeconomic status rather than merit Procedural Fairness The adjustment is opaque and not based on job relevant criteria Equity Creates barriers for outsiders non locals Group A who cannot access the local networking groups
